note: Synthesis of six biome proposals + three world proposals, filtered through three adversarial judges and re-verified against source. Every sprite claim below was checked against docs/asset-catalog/characters.json AND the file's existence under assets/time-fantasy/. `verified` records how. Nothing here is speculative unless `unconfirmed` is present.

sheetRoots.note: VERIFIED against tools/build-assets.mjs (SRC = assets/time-fantasy; MOBS = Characters/TimeFantasy_Monsters/1x; UNSORTED = Unsorted). NOTE lich.png and skeletonarmy.png live in Unsorted/, NOT in the monsters dir — the OLD sprites they replace (monster_lich.png, monster4.png) are the ones in Characters/TimeFantasy_Monsters/1x/.

engineWork.title: stripBakedShadow() in tools/build-assets.mjs
engineWork.why: bandits_1.png has an opaque drop-shadow ellipse painted into every frame (rgb 48,64,72). PROVEN: the bottom 3 rows of every frame are pixel-identical across all 8 character cells while the silhouettes above differ. build-assets.mjs:130-140 wires all six bandit sprites with no strip step, and the engine casts a real per-frame sprite shadow into the entity depth map. The eight shipped Thornhollow bandits are standing on black coins right now.
algorithm: modal exact RGB among opaque pixels in the bottom 3 rows of every frame
mask = pixels of that colour that are that colour in EVERY character cell at the same (frame,x,y); for `single` layout sheets intersect across the 12 frames instead
assert the mask is one connected, left-right symmetric blob touching the bottom edge; warn loudly otherwise

appliesTo.note: RE-EXTRACT the six shipped bandit sprites

engineWork.verifiedBy: two MMO_SHOT frames at different MMO_TIME_LOCK values — a baked disc does not rotate with the sun

engineWork.title: MobRankSchema disposition override
engineWork.cost: ~30 lines across registry.ts + mobs.ts + 2 vitest; no wire change, no client change
engineWork.why: MobRankSchema (registry.ts:205-217) exposes only add/remove/hpMult/damageMult/moveSpeedMult/titleSuffix. mobs.ts reads def.aggroRadius (:201), def.fleeAtHpPct (:212), the leash check and chooseAttack's attackRange arg DIRECTLY off the def. Ranks can change a mob's numbers and its buttons but never its NERVE. Four agents and three judges converged on this independently.

engineWork.title: Summon hygiene: grantsXp / grantsLoot + registry cross-check
engineWork.cost: ~15 lines in the death path + one registry test
engineWork.registryTest: TWO assertions, and the second must be written PRECISELY. (a) a summon's `mob` may not equal the summoner's own id. (b) resolveMob(childDef, childDef.level, scaling).attacks must contain no ability with a `summon` payload — i.e. resolve the child AT ITS BASE LEVEL, which is exactly what summonWave spawns. Do NOT write the naive version ('the child's kit, including every rank, may not contain a summon'): it produces a FALSE POSITIVE on grelmoss -> fen_slime, whose slime_split is an L14 rank that a base-L8 summoned slime can never unlock. I hit that false positive running this exact check against this roster.
engineWork.why: summonWave stamps summonerId on each minion (room.ts:491) so minionCountOf caps PER SUMMONER (room.ts:458-461). A slime whose split summons itself gives each child its own cap: exponential, and every child pays full XP and loot.
engineWork.verifiedGoodNews: summonWave calls spawnMob(mobId, x, z, "") with NO level argument (room.ts:489), so minions always spawn at the def's base level. Grelmoss's mire_spawn therefore raises L8 fen_slimes, which do not have slime_split (an L14 rank).

engineWork.title: cache_desert + cache_desert_poor + cache_dungeon in shared/loot.json
engineWork.why: VERIFIED ABSENT. hooks.lootCache table "auto" resolves to cache_<roomId> with a hardcoded cache_forest fallback (room.ts:578-582). Every desert and dungeon prefab cache in the game pays out FOREST loot today. Existing: cache_forest, cache_gloomfen, cache_cinderrift, cache_crypt, cache_royal.

engineWork.title: `sway` field on blocks.json
engineWork.detail: blocks.json `sway?: boolean` default `!glow`; BlockRegistry reads it; ChunkMesher.java:122 becomes `boolean sway = b.sway;` (currently `boolean sway = !b.glow;`).
engineWork.why: Every non-glow cross block sways in the wind. Without this, a funerary chain, a pile of skulls and a set of roots all wave gently in the breeze.

engineWork.title: Builder.digFloorY(groundY, depth) = Math.max(2, groundY - depth)
engineWork.why: Desert groundY runs 7..19 (base 13, amplitude 6) over bedrock at y1, and there is no min-ground-height scatter filter. Anything digging punches through the world at a low-ground site.

engineWork.title: Widen the client flame-flipbook world scan
engineWork.detail: WorldScreen keys the torch flame flipbook on `id == torch`. Widen to an emissive-flame set {torch, lantern, brazier}. ParticleField.setTorches already takes an arbitrary list.

engineWork.title: recolorIf(png, predicate, rgb) in tools/build-assets.mjs
engineWork.cost: ~6 lines; precedent = the existing chromaKey / tint helpers

engineWork.title: Builder.plate(x, y0, z, axis, rows[], legend)
engineWork.detail: A vertical ASCII-art block stamper, rotation-aware, '.' = skip. The difference between authoring the Colossus's face as 300 hand-placed ctx.set calls and as a 14-line string array a human can read in a diff.

deferredEngineAsks.title: hoverM on MobDef (billboard + entity-shadow-quad Y offset)
deferredEngineAsks.cost: ~half a day, client-only, no protocol change (key it off the sprite name the way AudioEngine already does)
deferredEngineAsks.consequence: There is no hovering movement. Once a hovering sprite is alpha-trimmed it sits on the ground: the djinn are buried to the waist, the skulls roll. This gates ~10 sheets: geniea_1, genieb_1, femaledjinn_1, beholder_a/b, bigskullA-D, harpy_a/b_fly. FOUR desert mobs were cut for it (lamp_thrall, sun_gazer, sun_skull, vashir_boss). Highest-leverage outstanding item.
deferredEngineAsks.ifLanded: bring back lamp_thrall (geniea_1) rebound as one of Ashkaal's bound tomb-spirits — 'it heals with its arms still folded, because it was never asked whether it wanted to' is the best character beat proposed.

deferredEngineAsks.title: payloadMult on MobRankSchema (scale allyHeal.amount / debuff.dotTotal / summon.count)
deferredEngineAsks.deferredBecause: The proposals contained TWELVE `_greater` abilities whose only diff from their parent was two numbers. This roster ships ZERO new ones. Revisit when a fourth appears.

deferredEngineAsks.title: pillars picks its FX strip from ability.fx
deferredEngineAsks.cost: one client lookup + one strip per element
deferredEngineAsks.deferredBecause: Every pillars ability here is fire (magma_vents, ember_trail) in fire rooms, alongside the shipped throne_flames and fuse_line. Nothing needs it yet.

deferredEngineAsks.title: wander: false sentry flag on MobDef
deferredEngineAsks.cost: one branch in tickBrain's patrol state
deferredEngineAsks.deferredBecause: forge_ward fakes it with aggroRadius 4, which is 80% of the effect. Without it the 'statues' on the forge gate visibly pace. Cheap; take it if you're already in mobs.ts for E1.

deferredEngineAsks.title: A `venom` projectile/impact strip
deferredEngineAsks.consequence: caustic_gob and sovereign_gob render as blue ice bolts (fx: frost) until it exists. A slime spitting an ice bolt is a lie the player notices in thirty seconds. Highest value-per-line art item outstanding.

trueClaims: interruptible:true on a mob ability works — interruptIfCasting gates on the ability flag alone, before any kind check (combat.ts:117-126), called from applyDamage (room.ts:1599)
an interrupt REFUNDS the cooldown (combat.ts:121) -> interrupting is tempo, not a lockout
DoT bites take their own path and cannot interrupt a cast
allyHeal with radius 2.5 + includeSelf is a gated SELF-heal for zero engine work; the option only enters the kit while an eligible ally is hurt (room.ts:1332-1341)
pillars is gated on the MOB's attackRange exactly like a projectile (mobs.ts:104-106) -> pillars from trash works, and ALREADY SHIPS (fuse_line on powder_brigand's L8 rank)
aggroRadius 0 -> never initiates (mobs.ts:201: threat <= 0 && d > aggroRadius)
fleeAtHpPct tests strict `<` behind a `> 0` guard (mobs.ts:212): 1.0 flees after any damage, 1.05 flees at full health
a mana ability on a mob returns false BEFORE setting a cooldown (combat.ts:44-51) -> chooseAttack re-picks it every tick -> permanent whiff loop
MobRankSchema cannot change aggroRadius / fleeAtHpPct / attackRange / leashRadius (registry.ts:205-217)
cache_desert does not exist; "auto" falls back to cache_forest (room.ts:578-582)
non-glow cross blocks sway in the wind (ChunkMesher.java:122) and are non-solid
PrefabDef.noClear exists; lootCache.local[1] is added to groundY, so negative Y offsets are legal (prefabs.ts:83, 266)
spawn-table entries carry an optional `level` that scales the def and unlocks ranks (rooms.ts:64-76)
blocks.json has TWO fields: kind (cube|cross) and cull (none|opaque|liquid|cutout)
a melee-only kit standing outside its own reach yields inRange=false -> {kind:'close'} (mobs.ts:98-113), so a WIDE attackRange is free to pre-author on a melee mob

falseClaims.claim: MobAttack.damage never scales — a latent bug at room.ts:1301
falseClaims.truth: FALSE. resolveMob (registry.ts:314-321) already multiplies every per-attack `damage` override by dmgMult, with a comment explaining why. room.ts:1299 reads the already-scaled value. Two of three judges repeated this. Do not 'fix' it.

falseClaims.claim: the first interruptible mob cast in the game
falseClaims.truth: FALSE. mend_kin and mend_kin_greater already ship with interruptible:true + allyHeal, carried by hollow_cowl's L10/L13 ranks (commit 1a417c3). Claimed independently by five agents.

falseClaims.claim: the Thornhollow Company is a proposal
falseClaims.truth: FALSE. bandit (= Thornhollow Cutthroat), greenhood_poacher, powder_brigand, bandit_enforcer, hollow_cowl, thrace_redcap, camp_cur, stolen_goat are all in shared/mobs.json. gloomfen.json already carries a `drowned-company` table that respawns them at L11-12.

falseClaims.claim: bandits_1.png decomposes cleanly (implying it is clean)
falseClaims.truth: It decomposes cleanly ON THE GRID. Nobody alpha-scanned it. See E0.

settledWithPixels.hieroglyph_wall: Two agents disagreed: aligned cell [10,24] vs off-grid (160,391). BOTH WRONG. I swept the window and measured the largest internal row-to-row brightness jump (a cornice band shows as a huge jump) and the tiling wrap seam: y=384 (aligned) wrapSeam 71.3 / maxInternalJump 99.7 (cornice band inside the tile); y=389 (+5) wrapSeam 15.4 / maxInternalJump 11.9 (clean glyph panel); y=391 (+7) wrapSeam 44.8 / maxInternalJump 35.5 (catches the sill band). LOCKED: grab(sheet, 160, 389, 16, 16), 256/256 opaque.
settledWithPixels.brazier: The prop spans (37,50)-(38,50) (~31 px). A 16-px window sweep found the symmetric window is exactly cell [38,50] (symmetry error 0, 187/256 opaque); it clips ~7 px of rim wing per side. Do NOT squash 31 px into 16 — it distorts the flame.

Must be hand-authored (precedent: painted web, painted glass). If the artist balks, cut `chain` and every prefab beat that leans on it (roadside_gibbet, sunken_gaol, charnel_scaffold, warding_ring, ossuary_barrow).

(34,14)-(35,14) = 'mossy round stones with a dark socket - read as skulls / eyed stones'. Alpha-clean (167/256, 164/256). Render both cells at 8x before compositing. If they read as boulders, use bone_block heaps and cut the id.
There is no green projectile strip. They ship as `frost` (blue). See deferred ask ART.

Catalog is emphatic: SINGLE layout, 3x4 of 64x48 frames (index.json's char8 guess slices every horse in half). Every mob billboard shipped so far has been squarish. /spawnmob cinder_nightmare + one screenshot BEFORE Batch 7. Ten minutes.
32x36 (96x144 sheet), unlike reaper_1's 26x36 (78x144). Do not share a frame width.
Catalog: a dark seam runs down ONE edge -> visible on two faces of a cube. Reads as a shadow groove. Ship it.

blocksNote: blocks.json ids are APPEND-ONLY (persisted room edits store raw ids). Next free id is 56. Thirty-five proposed names collapsed to these twenty-two; three agents probed the same ruindungeons sheet and each named the same pixels differently. `kind` and `cull` are SEPARATE fields — all 35 proposals wrote "cull":"cube". Every new id also needs its avgColor literal appended to the baked palette in server/shard/src/sim/voxel.ts (renderTopDown) or the admin live-map paints it magenta. `sway` requires E4.

blocks.name: pale_ruin_stone
blocks.label: Pale Ruin Stone
blocks.why: Pale sage-grey ashlar with fine diagonal crack hatching. Ysmere's stone. Paired with pale_temple_brick it gives decay a MATERIAL vocabulary instead of only hash bites: intact courses are brick, ruined courses are cracked stone.

blocks.name: pale_temple_brick
blocks.label: Pale Temple Brick
blocks.why: Bone-white ashlar, grey-green mortar. Cold pale masonry is a genuine gap: marble (44) is a polished imperial floor, stone_bricks (10) is warm castle grey. Use [42,15] as an alternate on long walls to break repetition.

blocks.name: crypt_slate
blocks.label: Crypt Slate
blocks.why: Blue-grey square floor tiles, dark grout, cold and institutional. Its real job: a cold blue-grey floor makes ankle-deep murk_water read as a WOUND rather than as terrain.

blocks.name: pale_fluted_column
blocks.label: Fluted Column
blocks.caveat: a dark seam runs down ONE edge -> visible on two faces of a cube; reads as a shadow groove
blocks.why: A 1x1 that reads as a column. We have never had one; it is the cheapest silhouette in Minecraft-language building. The best use is horizontal: a toppled six-long run lying in the murk that you walk across as a bridge.

blocks.name: rune_plate
blocks.label: Rune Plate
blocks.why: Self-framing, so a floor of them reads as individual seals rather than a smeared texture. ONE id, everywhere — the ward you learn in the fen must be the same stone you find cracked open on the temple altar and set into the lip of the Throat. (Merged: ward_plate, tomb_seal.) The eight sibling glyphs are all opaque and available for a future pressure-plate puzzle.

blocks.name: rune_plate_lit
blocks.label: Burning Ward
blocks.why: DARKEN THE STONE FIRST, then recolour only the glyph — glow:true full-brights the whole cube, so a full-bright pale plate just looks washed out. That is why stained_glass (55) works. light 7 sits under crystal (11) so it POOLS rather than lights a room. One block is the entire 'a ward was set here' vocabulary.

blocks.name: moss_carpet
blocks.label: Moss Carpet
blocks.why: Opaque corner-to-corner living moss (mossy_cobblestone (11) is stone with a green wash). A floor of it says NOBODY HAS WALKED HERE IN A CENTURY. Also the only saturated colour in a grey-brown biome.

blocks.name: hanging_moss
blocks.label: Hanging Moss
blocks.verified: my alpha scan: 101/256 opaque, spans rows 0..15 (hangs and stacks); [34,12] also full-height for a 2-block fall. Catalog range (32,11)-(35,14) = 'hanging green vines and moss tufts'
blocks.why: A REAL alpha strand anchored at the cell's top edge. Our vines (31) is a vertically-flipped grass tuft, tinted — serviceable and plainly a hack. Inherits the existing cross-plant wind sway for free.

blocks.name: roots
blocks.label: Roots

source.note: flipV + tint are both existing helpers; `vines` uses exactly this pair

blocks.verified: source cell is full-height, so the flip stays full-height and stacks
blocks.why: Reads two ways depending on placement: prising up through a crypt_slate floor from below, or groping down through a cracked ceiling. The block that makes a stone room feel INVADED rather than merely old. sway:false — roots do not wave.

blocks.name: skull_pile
blocks.label: Skulls

source.recipe: overlay(a, b, dx -3, dy +1) so it reads as a heap, not a single specimen

blocks.unconfirmed: the catalog hedges: 'mossy round stones with a dark socket - READ AS skulls / eyed stones'. Render both cells at 8x before compositing.
blocks.why: There is no ossuary without bones on the floor, and bone_block (40) is a wall material, not a pile. The moss on them is the good part — these are OLD dead, not fresh dead, and the difference is a whole century of story.

blocks.name: chain
blocks.label: Chain

source.recipe: vertical column of interlocking ovals, link pitch 5px so 3 links fill the tile and stacking is continuous; steel [139,143,150], outline [60,64,70], one highlight pixel on each link's left shoulder; ~35% coverage, HARD alpha (cutout, not blend)

blocks.unconfirmed: NO SOURCE TILE EXISTS on this sheet — the c0-23 catalog says so outright and I found none in c24-48. Hand-author it (precedent: painted web, painted glass, the shadow-map dump). If the artist balks, cut it and every prefab beat that leans on it.
blocks.why: One chain hanging over a rune_plate says 'something was bound here' louder than any block of masonry. Used by roadside_gibbet, sunken_gaol, charnel_scaffold, warding_ring, ossuary_barrow.

blocks.name: brazier
blocks.label: Brazier
blocks.verified: my window sweep: [38,50] is the symmetric 16-px window (symmetry error 0, 187/256 opaque). The prop spans (37,50)-(38,50) ~31px, so ~7px of rim wing clips per side. DO NOT squash 31px into 16 — it distorts the flame.
blocks.requires: E6 (widen the flame-flipbook scan to {torch, lantern, brazier})
blocks.why: A fire-bowl already drawn burning. light 13 sits just under torch (14). Its ABSENCE is the design: place the bowl's block as air, leave the soot, and the room says the tenders are dead.

blocks.name: temple_boards
blocks.label: Painted Boards
blocks.verified: catalog blockCandidate (pale_plank_boards); pixel-dumped 4px lossless period in both axes
blocks.trap: every c44/c46 cell of a c44-46 panel has a vertical trim post baked in; only c45 is clean
blocks.why: Cream-and-steel-blue FINISHED carpentry. Our planks (8) are raw honest lumber; these are painted. Someone painted these boards, and then the water came.

blocks.name: rotting_planks
blocks.label: Rotting Planks
blocks.note: the catalog's [45,15] is `pale_ruined_masonry`, NOT boards — one agent misread it. Prefer the in-pipeline tint: zero ambiguity, guaranteed to tile with the existing planks it abuts.
blocks.why: Lets the causeway decay MATERIALLY as it runs north (pale_ruin_stone -> planks -> rotting_planks) instead of only by hash-driven missing boards — a mechanic pretending to be a story becomes a story.

blocks.name: sewer_brick
blocks.label: Sewer Brick
blocks.why: Dark maroon. Nothing in the 56 shipped blocks is this colour: dark_bricks (35) is cold blue-black, cracked_bricks (51) is Valdrenn grey. Under lantern light it goes properly Victorian-catacomb.

blocks.name: dungeon_masonry
blocks.label: Dungeon Masonry
blocks.why: Warm brown coursed stacked stone with heavy shadow gaps. The generic 'somebody built this to hold people' block, and the natural mate for iron_bars (48). Distinct from cobblestone (9), which is rounded and grey. (Merged: tomb_brick.)

blocks.name: sewer_sludge
blocks.label: Sludge
blocks.why: Costs nothing: every runtime liquid check is kind-driven (cull=="liquid") and rooms select by name via terrain.liquid, so wading and swimming come free. Its payoff is a map cue — everything downstream of the outfall is sludge, everything upstream is fen. Mobs already wade purposefully, so a sludge channel is a real tactical feature.

blocks.name: bog_candle
blocks.label: Bog Candle

source.recipe: in-pipeline recolour toward sickly green-white; flame flipbook tinted green

blocks.why: The THIRD light word. torch/lantern = someone lives here (warm). crystal = danger + loot (cold blue). Corpse-light = someone WAS here and something is still burning for them. It rhymes with marsh_wisp: the wisps ARE the lamplighters, carrying the colour they left in the lamps. light 8 — brighter than nothing, never bright enough to feel safe.

blocks.name: sandstone_tomb_brick
blocks.label: Tomb Brick
blocks.trap: the row just above (r31) looks like brick but carries the pediment diagonal; r33 carries the cream basecourse. Use r32.
blocks.why: Gold-sandstone ashlar. Distinct from sandstone (12) the way brick is distinct from rock: it says SOMEBODY CUT THIS. The skin of the Colossus, the pylons, the tomb's outer shell, the aqueduct deck. (Merged: gold_tomb_brick.)

blocks.name: hieroglyph_wall
blocks.label: Hieroglyph Wall
blocks.verified: MY SEAM SWEEP. The aligned cell [10,24] ships a cornice band inside the tile (maxInternalJump 99.7). (160,389) is 256/256 opaque with maxInternalJump 11.9 and wrapSeam 15.4 — the cleanest window in the sweep. See verificationLedger.settledWithPixels.
blocks.why: Gold wall panel carved with standing-figure hieroglyphs in a recessed frame. THE storytelling block: every tomb corridor, every pylon face, every fragment's name-tablet. It IS the desert's 'architecture gold' — which is why gold_frieze was cut and gold_block (54) stays treasure. (Merged: hieroglyph_brick.)

blocks.name: sandstone_bricks
blocks.label: Mudbrick
blocks.why: Brown coursed ashlar — the living civilisation's cheap material as opposed to the god-kings' gold. Having a poor-man's masonry AND a king's masonry is what makes Ashkaal read as a SOCIETY rather than a ruin generator. Caravanserai walls, aqueduct piers, cistern rim.

blocks.name: sand_with_slab
blocks.label: Buried Slab
blocks.why: Rippled sand with a half-buried stone slab. Lay it in a scatter ring around every buried structure and the sand suddenly LOOKS like it is swallowing something. Highest anti-emptiness work per byte in the batch, and zero worldgen risk (structure-only).

blocksCut.name: ward_plate / tomb_seal
blocksCut.reason: both are rune_plate [22,4] (tomb_seal is its +15 gold twin at [22,19] — I confirmed [22,19] is also 256/256 opaque). ONE id: the glyph must be recognisable across rooms, which is the entire mechanic.

blocksCut.name: gold_frieze
blocksCut.reason: cited cell [5,24] is the TOP HALF of a 2-tall wall; the catalog's own trap list says the building-set brick wall is only two rows per theme and the lower carries a basecourse stripe. It cannot tile alone. The three-tier gold vocabulary is already covered: hieroglyph_wall (carved architecture) / sandstone_tomb_brick (ashlar) / gold_block 54 (treasure).

blocksCut.name: sandstone_fluted_column
blocksCut.reason: pale_fluted_column in a warm palette. One tile per idea.

blocksCut.name: rippled_sand
blocksCut.reason: its only interesting use (the desert biome surface) is correctly refused — the desert gen branch is test-locked byte-identical. That is a terrain.surfaceOverride decision on its own merits.

blocksCut.name: urn
blocksCut.reason: scatter dressing. Its best use is the ABSENCE of one: an air cell where an urn should be, with three rubble around it. That costs no id.

abilitiesNote: 28 new. ZERO new `_greater` abilities (the proposals contained twelve). Every reuse candidate was verified manaCost 0 — a mana ability on a mob is a permanent whiff loop (combat.ts:44-51). Convention enforced by bosses.test.ts: a projectile's maxRange must cover its carrier's attackRange; every kit below satisfies it. `summon.grantsXp/grantsLoot` require E2.

abilitiesMergedAway.params: dmg 7, minRange 4 — 650ms cast, spd 26, fx arrow. Ability ids are invisible to the player.

dmg 14 — 320ms windup, 1.9m, 85deg, canMoveWhile, dotTotal 10/4000ms. ALSO FIXES THE OBJECTION: a 140-200ms windup is not a telegraph, it is the removal of one, and this engine's combat contract is that the telegraphed windup IS the dodge window.
dmg 22 — 900/180/600, 2.2m, 100deg, rooted, cd 800. A bloatslime rearing.
dmg 1. Same trick stolen_goat already uses.
dmg override — 900ms cast, spd 18, max 18, fire.
same numbers; now shared by duneshadow_lioness, kaharat, forge_ward.

abilitiesMergedAway.proposed: every _greater (mend_company_greater, greater_bone_mend, wish_of_wholeness, forge_mend_greater, rime_shard_greater, slime_split_greater, greater_gaze, pride_roar_greater, slag_gorge_greater, powder_keg, ...)

summon.text: The Stitcher rasps: 'Back to your feet. The work is not done.'

abilities.feelsLike: the floor standing up

one word changed in a summon block, and you understand you have walked into the factory. At L12 it raises SCRAP; at L15, standing in Morvane's workshop, the identical creature raises DEATHLESS LEGIONARIES.
a dodgeable hook that sets up its own reap. Getting caught, and knowing exactly what lands next.
it tears its own ribcage off and throws it at you — the punishment for the correct instinct
a swing from a suit hot enough that the miss still burns. The DoT means the answer to a patrol is to break it up, not to tank it.

allyHeal.text: The Forge-Tender raises her tongs — the plate glows and knits.

abilities.designNote: interruptible: FALSE, DELIBERATELY. The deliberate mirror of slag_gorge. The game is not 'stop the heal' — it is 'she must not be alive when the heal comes up.' Two mobs, one engine field, two completely different player skills, fought within ten minutes of each other.
abilities.feelsLike: a fact you cannot argue with. Kill her.

abilities.designNote: pillars is gated on the MOB's attackRange exactly like a projectile (mobs.ts:104-106), and already ships from a trash mob (fuse_line on powder_brigand L8). Fire in a foundry is the one place a fire pillar is diegetic — which is why six of the eight proposed pillars abilities were cut rather than asking for E11.
abilities.feelsLike: you may not stand here
the ground getting further away

abilities.designNote: cannot reuse greater_frost: it costs 14 mana and mobs have no mana pool

allyHeal.text: The Slagback gulps molten slag; its wounds seal.

abilities.designNote: A GATED SELF-HEAL BUILT ENTIRELY OUT OF THE SHIPPED allyHeal PATH, zero engine work (verified room.ts:1332 only offers the option while an eligible ally is below the pct). radius 2.5 also catches a densely packed denmate, which is correct. Any single point of DIRECT damage breaks the cast and burns 9 seconds; DoT bites never interrupt, so poison cannot cheese it. DO NOT 'FIX' THIS LATER — there is nothing to add.
abilities.feelsLike: a window
a lunge that lands a metre past where melee is supposed to end. The REACH is the mechanic — it invalidates the reflex backpedal-during-the-telegraph. Sidestep beats backstep, which is the lesson.
it tears a piece off its own wing and leads your sprint with it. `predictive` is what makes retreating feel punished rather than merely slow.
you may not run in a straight line
a slow enormous sweep you can always see coming and can never quite un-see afterward. The DoT follows you into the next fight — the desert's whole thesis.
its own unravelling bandages, flung at you when you back off. The one mob you could always outwalk, you cannot.

summon.text: The Herald unwinds its jaw, and the skulls of its servants come loose.

abilities.designNote: summons `skeleton` — Ashkaal's levy dead, already living in the desert's spawn tables. skeleton has no summon, so no recursion (E2 test-locked).

abilities.feelsLike: enormous, slow, unmissable. A fair telegraph on an unfair animal.

summon.text: Kaharat's roar rolls across the dunes — the pride answers.

abilities.designNote: The engine drops a summon option out of the kit at cap and tops it back up below cap (room.ts:1327-1330). The fight teaches 'kill the caller' with zero new engine code.
abilities.feelsLike: the sound of losing
a slime folding over your legs. Low damage, and you can't leave.

abilities.fxWarning: THERE IS NO GREEN PROJECTILE STRIP. This renders as a blue ice bolt until a `venom` strip exists. See deferredEngineAsks.ART.

summon.text: The bloatslime sloughs off a piece of itself.

abilities.designNote: Summons a DISTINCT id whose kit contains no summon — the structural fix for the per-summoner cap. A 1200ms telegraphed split you can race, interrupt or pre-empt is a decision; an unavoidable on-death spawn is a tax. Deliberately not the Minecraft behaviour.
abilities.feelsLike: a telegraph you can race
the boss rises and comes down over a 170-degree arc. A full second of telegraph; the slow on hit means the second one lands.
it leads your tracked velocity and re-aims at release, so a juke beats it and a straight-line run does not

summon.text: Grelmoss shudders, and the mire stands up.

abilities.designNote: summonWave passes NO level (room.ts:489), so these are base-L8 fen_slimes and never carry slime_split (an L14 rank). Verified; test-locked by E2.

abilities.feelsLike: a lance, not a swing. Narrowest cone in the game — it can be sidestepped by anyone paying attention, and it deletes anyone who is not.

abilities.designNote: cannot reuse the shipped `heal` — it costs 20 mana. 180 per 8s is a hard dps floor: a solo low-level literally cannot out-damage it, and the game never says so.
abilities.feelsLike: the unicorn refusing to die
it does not stop to hit you. It hits you WHILE running you down, and you keep burning afterwards.
six hoofprints of fire walking through where you are about to be. Standing still burns, running straight burns, strafing lives.

mobsNote: 24 new. `spriteName` is the value of mobs.json's `sprite` field AND the key AudioEngine uses for vocals — a `sounds` block naming existing groups is therefore all that is needed (build-sounds.mjs fails the build on an unresolved ref, so a wrong name is caught). `spriteSource` is what goes into tools/build-assets.mjs SHEETS. PRE-AUTHORED attackRange: a melee-only kit standing outside its own reach yields {kind:'close'} (mobs.ts:98-113), so a WIDE attackRange is free at base level and is what makes a rank-unlocked projectile fire the day it unlocks. IF ANYONE LATER 'TIDIES' THOSE VALUES DOWN TO MELEE REACH, EVERY RANGED RANK HERE SILENTLY STOPS FIRING. `rankDisposition` requires E1.

mobs.name: Restless Bones
mobs.verified: catalog entry exists; file exists; 'Bare bone-WHITE skeleton, naked, no gear, empty hands, big round skull with black sockets and a tooth row.' Catalog: skeletonarmy.png is a clean 8-character sheet with 3x4 walk grids in BOTH halves.
mobs.why: It exists to be worthless, AND THAT IS A DESIGN POSITION. It is what raise_bones conjures; it makes the crypt floor read as an industrial by-product rather than a monster closet; and its emptiness is what makes an armed, helmed Skeleton Legionary land as an upgrade rather than a palette swap. You cannot see the assembly line without seeing the parts.

mobs.name: Ossuary Stitcher
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Dark grey robe with red/orange piping, no hood; large bare WHITE skull head with big black eye sockets sitting atop the robe. Simplest of the four robed variants.' File exists. Catalog: no off-grid/garbled halves in lich.png.
mobs.why: Deliberately CROWNLESS — the other three robed figures on lich.png wear gold crowns and gold ribcages. Crowns are for Morvane. This one is staff. One sprite cell, and the faction hierarchy draws itself. mend_kin is interruptible:true, so a real hit staggers it mid-cast; DoT bites bypass interrupts, so poison will NOT stop it — you have to walk through the pack and swing. And the interrupt refunds the cooldown, so interrupting is TEMPO, not a lockout. Kill it, or fight it forever.

mobs.name: Bone Warden
mobs.verified: catalog entry exists (heightM 2.6): 'the same stone warden with BONE instead of crystal: ivory-white curved horns, ivory rib-shards...'. File exists. heightM 2.6 has precedent (sundered_king 2.6).
mobs.why: Bright green moss creeping over its arms. Not Morvane's work — he FOUND it. The moss says it predates the whole operation and nothing has cleaned it off. Mixed melee+ranged with attackRange 20 means the engine closes it toward melee between shots: backing up is not an answer, it just changes which attack you eat. boss_slam's 1300ms rooted telegraph is the correct antithesis to this batch's swarms.

mobs.name: Grave Harrower
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Identical hooded skull reaper to reaper_1, but WIELDING A SCYTHE'. WARNING (catalog): reaper_blade_1.png uses a WIDER 32x36 frame (96x144 sheet) than reaper_1.png's 26x36 (78x144) — do not reuse the same frame width for both. File exists.
mobs.why: At L12 it raises SCRAP. At L15, standing in Morvane's workshop, the identical creature raises DEATHLESS LEGIONARIES. One word changed in a summon block, and the biome's whole thesis lands. THE FREE LORE: the Harrower carries a scythe; the Wraiths (reaper_1, the identical creature minus the scythe) do not. Morvane arms the ones he trusts.

mobs.name: Crypt Ghoul
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Emaciated hunched ghoul: sickly pale grey-tan skin with visible ribs, wild grey-lavender hair...' (heightM 1.6). File exists.
mobs.changedFromProposal: The original wanted a 200ms windup at cd 200, escalating to 140ms at cd 0. Its own author wrote 'there is no dodge window; there is only killing it first' — an argument for why the mob shouldn't exist. Shipped spider_bite (320ms, cd 300, canMoveWhile, dot 10/4000) delivers the whole fantasy and honours the engine's combat contract. ghoul_rake / ghoul_frenzy / venom_flurry are cut.
mobs.why: spider_bite is canMoveWhile, so it rakes you WHILE chasing, at 4.4 m/s, in packs of 3-4 — and the engine's 0.45m separation fans them into a closing ring rather than a blob. The counter is never to let them surround you, i.e. to fight in a corridor. Never spawn in the same region as a Stitcher: ghouls are what happens when nobody stitches you.

mobs.name: Pallid Mourner
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Classic sheet ghost: rounded pale blue-white blob body, two small dark oval eyes...'. File exists. I ALPHA-SCANNED IT: rows y31-y35 are a solid rgb(53,64,72) shadow ellipse welded into the sprite. REQUIRES E0.
mobs.why: These are the crypt's ACTUAL dead — the ones Morvane hasn't gotten to yet. They drift between the bone racks and they run from you. They give nothing. Within ten minutes the player stops noticing them. Then, in the Vaults: same little ghost, same three dark holes in its face, AND IT DOES NOT RUN. Nothing costs less and lands harder than a thing the player learned was safe. It is also the mob that PROVES E1 — without the disposition override it is a ghost that runs away forever, and the recognition beat, which is its entire reason to exist, does not exist.

mobs.name: Ember Warplate
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Crimson armored elemental knight. Faceless helmet with a tall U-shaped red crest, red plate over gray metal limbs, no visible eyes or mouth.' Catalog: warelementals_1 decomposes cleanly into 8 x (3x4), no off-grid junk half. File exists.
mobs.soundDirection: its hurt sound should be a BELL, not a voice; its die sound a pile of plate hitting stone
mobs.why: An empty suit. The guild's actual product: armour that needs no soldier inside it. These are the ones still walking the guard rotation for a guild that has been ash for two hundred years — AND THEY STILL LIGHT THE LAMPS ALONG THEIR ROUTE, so the only lit paths in the valley are the ones they walk. That is the light vocabulary promoted from prefab dressing to room-scale layout, and the one place a MOB authors the map. At L16 the chip DoT is REMOVED for a real 1200ms telegraph: more dangerous and more readable at once.

mobs.name: Forge-Tender
mobs.role: healer — KILL THIS FIRST, AND YOU CANNOT INTERRUPT HER
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Gray-armored female with a large gold curved horn-loop headdress (like a lyre or crescent), gold belt and trim, orange glow at the hips.' Catalog: warelementals_f_1 decomposes cleanly. File exists.
mobs.why: forge_mend is interruptible:FALSE, DELIBERATELY, as the mirror of the Slagback Troll's trivially-interruptible self-heal. Two mobs, one engine field, two completely different player skills: 'she must not be alive when the heal comes up' vs 'hold one swing and break the cast.' allyHeal only enters her kit while an ally is under 80%, so she opens the fight politely bolting you, and the instant you hurt a Warplate she starts working. 90hp is a third of a Warplate. The L14 rank kills the low-rank answer ('sprint past the plate and stand on the healer') without changing a single number: now the floor under her erupts.

mobs.name: Frostplate Revenant
mobs.role: rare roaming elite — changes how every pack fights
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Blue armored knight, tall sharply pointed crested helm like a shark fin, spiky ice-blue plate with gold buckles at the waist.' File exists.
mobs.why: The guild's one failed binding: something from under the ice climbed into the plate instead of fire. It has been walking out of this valley for two hundred years. IT KILLS THE RED SUITS TOO. The engine has no ally damage buffs, so this mob buffs the pack through THE ONLY STAT THE PLAYER OWNS: a 45% slow means the 1.9 m/s ash husks you were trivially kiting finally catch you. It contributes nothing and doubles the pack's lethality. leash 60 because it has no home, so it follows you INTO other packs. And one blue silhouette in a red valley is a question the player asks out loud before they check its health bar.

mobs.name: Slagback Troll
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Identical rig and pose set to troll1a, recolored: slate blue-gray / pale teal...' (heightM 2.4). CATALOG WARNING: both trolls are drawn UNARMED — any weapon must come from FX/projectiles, not the sprite. Correct here: the claws are the weapon. File exists.
mobs.spawnHint: bind to the shipped mine_adit prefab via bindSpawnTable, the way bandits bind to bandit_fort
mobs.why: It eats the cooling slag off the runoff channels; the metal in its blood closes its wounds. The disaster made it fat. It kneels down and eats molten slag for 1.5s, and ANY SINGLE POINT OF DIRECT DAMAGE breaks the cast and burns a 9-second cooldown. DoT bites never interrupt, so a poison ticking on it does NOT cheese the mechanic — you have to hold a swing. Solo you learn to save a hit; in a group it never lands at all, which is the correct tax on bringing friends.

mobs.name: Forge-Ward
mobs.role: sentry / area denial — THE FIGHT YOU ARE ALLOWED TO DECLINE
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Stone-gray winged demon in a crouch. Broad snarling head with two curled ram-like horns...' (heightM 2.0). File exists.
optionalEngine: E12 wander:false — without it the 'statues' visibly pace on the gate
mobs.mergedFrom: tomb_warden (desert) — same sheet, same crouch, same aggroRadius-as-statue gimmick, proposed twice. Cinderrift's fiction is better integrated; the desert's kit is better. This is both.
mobs.spawnHint: on the forge_ruin gate and the top spans of ruined_aqueduct — sitting directly on the loot caches
mobs.why: No eyes — blank stone. Carved BEFORE the Emberwrights learned to bind fire: the cheap solution, stone that simply hates trespassers. aggroRadius 4 is the whole gimmick: the valley is dressed with gargoyle statues, and some of them are not statues. It is the only mob in the Cinderrift you can WALK AWAY FROM, and that is its entire mechanic. It also makes ARCHITECTURE dangerous, so the player starts reading buildings instead of the ground. At L15 the decline stops being available — WHICH IS EXACTLY WHY E1 EXISTS. The original proposal's L15 rank silently broke its own contract, because ranks cannot widen leashRadius.

mobs.name: Forge Prototype
mobs.role: elite — punishes standing still AND kiting
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Dark charcoal-gray rock body cracked with molten orange fissures, gold horn/wing plates sweeping off the head, chunky lava-lit limbs.' NOT the shipped cinder_golem_boss sprite (that is monster_golem1.png, a plain grey stone golem). File exists.
mobs.renamedFrom: cinder_colossus — the shipped boss is `cinder_golem_boss` and two 'colossus' ids in one room is a support ticket
mobs.conventionCheck: attackRange 15 <= slag_lob.maxRange 28 OK; magma_vents is pillars, gated on attackRange OK
mobs.spawnHint: spawn THREE, maxAlive 1 each, standing exactly where they were left in the yard
mobs.why: The Furnace Golem's prototypes. Three of them, smaller and cruder, downed tools in the yard the day the big one worked. Nobody scrapped them. Nobody told them anything. Its two casts close both escape hatches: magma_vents marches a line of fire THROUGH where you are standing, and slag_lob leads your velocity so backpedalling in a straight line eats a 2.6m splash. You have to strafe and re-close. Seeing three of them abandoned BEFORE you reach the Furnace Golem makes the boss reveal land: oh — that's the one that worked. At L16 it throws the Sundered King's own hazard: a quiet implication about who the Emberwrights were selling to.

mobs.name: Sandpicker
mobs.verified: catalog: 'RED-skinned goblino, bare, with a bright orange flame-shaped mohawk tuft of hair. No clothing at all.' Catalog: goblinos decomposes cleanly into 8 x (3x4). File exists.
mobs.why: ZERO new abilities. The mixed kit fans a five-pack out — three knife-runners, two hanging back throwing rocks — and flee 0.30 means the fight has a SHAPE (break them) rather than a DURATION (grind them). Not part of the story — the EVIDENCE of it. Sandpickers tunnelled into the necropolis for the gold. Every broken tomb door in Sunscour is theirs. They are why the dead are walking, and they have no idea. At L12 it trades the sling for a crossbow prised off a dead garrison soldier.

mobs.name: Withered Courtier
mobs.role: tank / attrition wall — THE RANK THAT CHANGES WHAT A MOB MEANS
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Humanoid entirely wrapped head-to-toe in dirty off-white/grey bandage strips; NO face...' (heightM 1.75). File exists. Stands on the ground — no hover.
mobs.why: The court itself. They are not hostile the way a wolf is hostile — THEY ARE STILL PERFORMING THE FUNERAL RITES, AND YOU ARE STANDING IN THE PROCESSION. At L6 the room's only true wall, and not an hp bag: at 1.2 m/s you can always walk away, and it does not care, because the rot is already in you. At L9 it flings its own unravelling bandages at you — 55% slow — and the mob you could always outwalk suddenly cannot be outwalked. The pre-authored attackRange 12 is what makes that land the day it unlocks. At L12 the Herald unwinds its jaw and Ashkaal's levy dead come loose. Same sprite. Four lines of JSON.

mobs.name: Duneshadow Lioness
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Lioness: the same golden big cat with NO mane, small rounded ears, a slimmer head.' CATALOG WARNING: BAKED DROP SHADOW (dark blue-gray oval painted INTO the sprite). File exists. REQUIRES E0.
mobs.why: pounce (3.6m, 45deg, canMoveWhile) reaches a full metre past where melee is supposed to end, so the reflex backpedal-during-the-telegraph FAILS. Three of them at 4.4 m/s in open dunes with nothing to break line of sight: no kiting answer, no wall to back into. Sunscour has skeletons and cactos and no ecology. A pride means there is something out here worth eating, which means there is something out here that eats. Their kills become the carrion_nest prefab.

mobs.name: Kaharat, the Red Mane
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Male lion: golden-yellow big cat with a large shaggy amber mane framing a small [face]'. CATALOG WARNING: BAKED DROP SHADOW. File exists. REQUIRES E0.
mobs.spawnHint: holds the oasis (324,354) at night; maxAlive 1, respawnSec 600
mobs.why: He is the reason the desert's bones are picked clean. The first thing in the game that is a FIGHT rather than a stat block, and he is not a boss — he is just an animal that is winning. The engine drops a summon option out of the kit at cap and tops it back up below it, so the fight teaches itself with zero new engine code: kill lionesses forever and lose, or eat the lion_maul telegraphs, burn the lion down, and the pride evaporates. (pride_roar_greater is cut: a copy-paste ability whose only diff was count/cap.)

mobs.name: Sekhat the Ninth
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Bulkier death knight: dark steel-grey spiked helm with horns, teal-white bone-plate cuirass and huge pauldrons, gold/tan belt, brown boots. Row 3 (up) shows a wide white/grey cape.' (catalog heightM 1.9; authored at 2.1 — a king reads above his court; precedent: marauder 1.8, sundered_king 2.6). File exists.

actions.text: Sekhat rasps: The water is close. DIG.
The wellhead cracks. Far below, nothing moves.

mobs.replaces: vashir_boss (a legless hovering djinn — no hover exists; see E9)
mobs.why: ZERO new abilities beyond his court's. Not a caster: THE KING WHO ORDERED THE DIG, IN HIS WAR-PLATE, STILL HOLDING COURT. A bigger Courtier who can call the dead and swing like a boss, and every ability he has, his court has. Spawns in the Vessel Chamber of setpiece S4.

mobs.name: Glimmereye
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Bushbaby/galago: small gray-blue furred primate with HUGE round orange saucer eyes...'. Catalog: chameleon/platypus/badger/bushbaby/butterflyA/B/gorilla_1 all share the 42x36 frame (126x144 sheet) = single character, 3x4, all four rows on-grid and legible. File exists.
mobs.ranksNote: NONE, DELIBERATELY. Ranks cannot touch fleeAtHpPct or aggroRadius pre-E1, and a critter that grows teeth stops being a critter.
mobs.why: fleeAtHpPct 1.05 means hp/maxHp < 1.05 is true at FULL health (verified strict `<` at mobs.ts:212), so the instant it acquires you it turns and bolts, faster than you walk. The `punch` entry exists only because registry.ts rejects an empty kit. ITS EYES ARE THE EXACT ORANGE OF A TORCH FLAME. At 20m in the dark fen it reads as a lantern. You walk toward it. It bolts. AND YOU ARE NOW STANDING SOMEWHERE YOU DID NOT CHOOSE TO BE. It weaponises the shipped lighting model with one sprite and a number above 1.0. (Five other harmless critters were proposed. One ambient creature per outdoor room.)

mobs.name: Fen Slime
mobs.role: swarm / melee — the cheapest demonstration of ranks
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Small blue blob slime with a white highlight, same squash cycle.' CATALOG WARNING: slimevariants_1's char8 guess is HALF WRONG — only the TOP row of character blocks (0,0 / 1,0 / 2,0 / 3,0) contains art; the whole bottom row is empty transparent cells. Within each character all four direction rows are IDENTICAL and the three columns are a bounce cycle, not a walk. Correct for a blob; a naive char8 mapping indexes into transparency. File exists.
mobs.why: It doesn't hit hard — it holds you in place while everything else in the fen closes. At L11 it has learned to SPIT, so the mixed kit closes it toward melee between gobs and you can no longer just back up. At L14 one pull becomes four. Same JSON entry: fodder at the fen's southern edge, a genuine problem when the temple spawns it at 14, and the player learns to read the title prefix as a threat level.

mobs.name: Fen Slimeling
mobs.verified: reuses fen_slime's sprite at a smaller heightM
mobs.ranksNote: NO RANKS. NO SUMMON. EVER. This is enforced by the E2 registry cross-check.
mobs.why: summonWave stamps summonerId on each minion, so minionCountOf caps PER SUMMONER (room.ts:458-461). A slime whose slime_split summons ITSELF gives each child its own cap: exponential, and every child pays full XP and loot. Three proposed mobs were designed on top of that. The structural fix is that a splitter must summon a DIFFERENT id whose kit contains no summon — and every summon in this roster additionally sets grantsXp:false, grantsLoot:false. Belt and braces.

mobs.name: Bloatslime
mobs.role: elite / attrition — a DPS check disguised as a mob you can outwalk
mobs.verified: catalog: 'A large emerald-green slime: a fat rounded dome with a darker green base rim and [white highlights]'. CATALOG: slime_big_single / slime_king_single — 3x4 grid exists, but all 4 direction rows are IDENTICAL; the 3 columns are a squash/stretch bounce. Non-directional by design. File exists.
mobs.why: Where a Fen Slime ends up when nobody kills it. The temple nave is full of them, settled on the marble like sacks. It never chases you; it just gets bigger behind you. Every 12 seconds it is alive it spends casting a visible, telegraphed, INTERRUPTIBLE split. Kill it inside the window or the encounter compounds. A telegraph you can race, interrupt or pre-empt is a decision; an unavoidable on-death spawn is a tax. Deliberately not the Minecraft behaviour.

mobs.name: Grelmoss, the Crowned Mire
mobs.role: boss (open-world) — THE ANSWER TO 'WHAT HAPPENED HERE'
mobs.verified: catalog: 'A big golden-amber slime with white glossy highlights, wearing a small spiked orange-gold [crown]'. Non-directional bounce cycle. File exists. THE CROWN IS DRAWN INTO THE SPRITE.
mobs.newLootTable: grelmoss_drops — gold-heavy, guaranteed rare+, ancient_coin, AND `royal_seal` (the SAME trophy Vaelric drops)
mobs.soundDirection: pitch the slime vocal groups down

actions.text: Grelmoss shudders — the mire boils, and the fat ones rise.

mobs.why: Ysmere's last tax convoy to Valdrenn went into the water on the Drowned West Road when the causeway gave. Something in the murk ate the convoy, and ten years later it is still wearing the courier's crown, half-dissolved. grelmoss_drops includes royal_seal — THE SAME TROPHY VAELRIC DROPS, TWO ROOMS AWAY. The trophy is the receipt. A player who kills the Sundered King and then comes back here and finds the same seal on a slime has just been told a story nobody narrated at them. Highest story-per-byte device in the document; steal it for every biome. (mire_spawn raises fen_slime at its BASE L8 — summonWave passes no level — so they never split.)

mobs.name: Aelthir, the Unmarred
mobs.role: rare wandering neutral — THE MEMORABLE ENCOUNTER
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Unicorn: a tall pale white/ivory horse with a long straight white spiral horn...'. CATALOG WARNING: BAKED DROP SHADOW — the most obvious of any sheet in the group. File exists. REQUIRES E0.
mobs.ranksNote: L16 is authored for a future staged encounter and is spawned nowhere. The base def is meant to be met exactly once, at level four, in a sunbeam, and remembered.
mobs.newLootTable: unmarred_drops — guaranteed `spiral_horn` trophy, value ~600, the most valuable trophy in the game
mobs.why: aggroRadius 0 means it NEVER attacks first (mobs.ts:201 skips targets outside aggroRadius while threat <= 0). fleeAtHpPct 0 means it will not run, either. A level-12 creature with boss hp and 44 damage grazing in a level 1-7 room — and the game will absolutely let a level-4 player throw a rock at it and die to one horn_charge. 900hp behind a 180-point interruptible self-heal on an 8s cooldown is unkillable solo and clean for a group that interrupts. bossDeath fires on ANY death of that mob id, so the gravehound punishment costs zero engine work. Half the playerbase tells the story of the sunbeam. Half tells the story of what came out of the trees. Both are the same feature.

mobs.name: Cinder Nightmare
mobs.verified: catalog: 'Nightmare (demon horse): a steel-blue/slate horse with glowing pale-white slit eyes' + mane/tail/hooves of live fire. CATALOG WARNING #1: index.json guesses 'single+char8' — the char8 reading is WRONG and slices every horse in half. The coherent reading is SINGLE: 192x192 = 3 cols x 4 rows of 64x48 frames (wide because of the flame trail). CATALOG WARNING #2: BAKED DROP SHADOW. File exists.
mobs.unconfirmed: Every mob billboard shipped so far has come from squarish cells. VERIFY THE SPRITE LOADER HANDLES 64x48 BEFORE BUILDING A FIGHT ON IT: /spawnmob cinder_nightmare + one screenshot. Ten minutes.
mobs.conventionCheck: attackRange 14; ember_trail is pillars (attackRange-gated) OK; L17 sundering_wave maxRange 44 OK
mobs.why: moveSpeed 5.0 — the fastest creature in the game; outrunning it is not on the menu. nightmare_charge is canMoveWhile: it runs you down WHILE winding up and leaves you burning. ember_trail marches six pillars through your predicted position. Standing still burns. Running straight burns. Only strafing across the line survives. At L17, riderless in Valdrenn's burned market, it picks up the King's own predictive exploding wave: THE HORSE THAT SURVIVED HIM FIGHTS LIKE HIM. One def, two rooms, two completely different fights, and a piece of story told purely through a shared ability id. And when a player who has met Aelthir sees this thing come out of the ash, the recognition does the work no cutscene could.

reSpritesNote: KEEP THE `sprite` NAME and swap only the source file/cell in tools/build-assets.mjs. AudioEngine keys mob vocals by sprite NAME, so nothing orphans. (A judge warned the skeleton re-sprite would break its vocal groups. It only would if you renamed the sprite. Don't.) One line each.

reSprites.verified: catalog: 'Tan skeleton in a crested/domed steel helm and brown leather kilt, HOLDING a pale blue-white straight sword held out diagonally.'
reSprites.why: Reads as a SOLDIER, not a monster. That is the entire point of the four-rank ladder: the moment a 'skeleton' out-ranges your sword with a pike, the room has told you that something down here has been TRAINING them.

reSprites.verified: catalog: 'Hooded reaper: pale sage-green/grey hood and robe with a purple-black rim; a bon[e-white skull]'. reaper_blade_1 is described as the IDENTICAL creature 'but WIELDING A SCYTHE'.
reSprites.why: The Grave Harrower carries a scythe. The Wraiths don't. Identical creature, minus the scythe. Morvane arms the ones he trusts, and the player draws that conclusion unprompted from two sprites in the same wing. One line in build-assets.mjs and a faction hierarchy draws itself. Best cost/benefit ratio in the document.

reSprites.verified: catalog: 'Classic lich: dark blue-grey hooded robe, tall gold crown, glowing red eyes and red mouth in a skull face; bright GOLD skeletal ribcage/arm bones drawn over the robe; gold-gloved hands.'
reSprites.why: A dramatically better boss silhouette — and, crucially, it makes lich.png [3,1] (the crownless bare-white-skull Ossuary Stitcher) read on sight as HIS STAFF. Same sheet, same robe language, one wears a crown.

prefabsNote: 21, in the design doc's section-4a three-questions format (who made it / what happened / what's left to take). SEVEN carry a labelled `interrupted` action; the rest deliberately do not — eight of eight Gloomfen prefabs and eight of eight crypt prefabs shipped with one, and a good rule became a tic. When every ruin has a severed chain, no severed chain means anything. All hooks use existing machinery. Anything that digs uses E5's Builder.digFloorY.

prefabs.who: The Tidewardens — a priesthood of hydrologists.
prefabs.whatHappened: They sank rings of sealed sluice-stones across the fen and the marsh stayed out of Ysmere for four hundred years. The seals failed together, in one night, because someone broke the master seal on the temple altar.
prefabs.whatsLeft: A warden's satchel under the cracked cylinder.
prefabs.build: 3x3 pale_temple_brick cylinder, 3 tall, capped by a rune_plate slab with a blue_crystal on top. A ring of 8 rune_plate set FLUSH at y=groundY (inlaid, not stacked) at radius 4. moss_carpet in the interstices.

ruinLevels.0: INTACT — fill every column inside r4 up to waterLevel+1 with grass/mud, so the ward stands in A CIRCLE OF DRY GROUND WHILE MURK LAPS AT ITS EDGE. Crystal lit. Four small lantern posts at the compass points. Twenty lines of code for a genuinely beautiful thing to come across.
ruinLevels.1: CRACKED — one rune_plate missing from the ring; a tongue of murk_water reaches in through the gap; two lanterns out.
ruinLevels.2: SHATTERED — the cylinder is a 3x3 rubble stump, the crystal is obsidian, the disk is fully flooded and indistinguishable from the fen except for the ring of plates you can just see under the water.

prefabs.interrupted: At ruinLevel 1, a single rune_plate lies flat and loose on the dry disk, THREE BLOCKS INSIDE THE RING, moss already on it. It did not fall out. It was pried out, and dragged INWARD. Somebody sabotaged the seals from the inside. That one block is the entire plot of the Gloomfen.
prefabs.why: Six of these, read in decay order via the existing distance-driven ruin gradient, teach the player the temple's cracked altar BEFORE they ever reach it. Zero dialogue.

prefabs.placement: FIXED anchors along the causeway (see setpiece S3), not scattered
prefabs.who: The kingdom. Ysmere paid a corps of lamplighters to walk the causeway at dusk so the temple's pilgrims could find their way home.
prefabs.whatHappened: They walked it the night the marsh came up. The lamps went out, one by one, from the temple end back toward the gate.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Nothing. WAYSHRINE-CLASS: no loot cache. It is a promise, not a prize.
prefabs.build: Four pale_log piles to deckY = waterLevel+1. pale_ruin_stone plinth, pale_fluted_column shaft, an iron_bars cage at the top.

ruinLevels.1: bog_candle — the lamp went out, and SOMETHING ELSE lit it
ruinLevels.2: dark, and one iron_bars missing from the cage. A lantern block sits on the flooded ground at the post's foot at y=groundY, UNDER the murk, still lit, glowing up through the water. He dropped it and never picked it up. (light 13 through 1-2 blocks of murk_water is a strong, free, diegetic underwater glow.)

prefabs.why: The marsh_wisp IS the lamplighter, still walking his stretch of road, still holding his light — which is why bog_candle and the wisp are the same colour.

prefabs.who: A soldier of Ashkaal.
prefabs.whatHappened: He was told to hold this stretch of road. Nobody ever told him to stop.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Last month's pay, never spent.
prefabs.build: A 5-block sandstone breastwork arc, two tall, opening away from the nearest road/aqueduct leg. Two crossed log spears. A log pole flying a banner. Two bone_block at groundY+1..2 — he is still standing his post, upright, inside the breastwork. A cold ash fire ring.

ruinLevels.0: A TORCH BURNS ON THE POLE. The post is still manned. That is WORSE, not better, and it is the single best sentence this prefab writes.
ruinLevels.2: no torch; the banner is a hash-coin away from being gone

prefabs.why: Five blocks, two bones, a banner — and at count 8 / spacing 45 the dunes stop being terrain and become a frontier that somebody used to patrol. The answer to 'the world feels empty' is not more mobs. HE GETS UP.

prefabs.who: The god-kings. This is a temple gate.
prefabs.whatHappened: The district it opened onto is under forty feet of sand.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Whatever a robber wedged into the lintel void and never came back for.
prefabs.build: Two battered towers, buried 4 below groundY, rising 7 above, tapering inward above +4. Cores sandstone_tomb_brick; the two OUTWARD faces faced with hieroglyph_wall. A lintel spanning the gap with a single gold_block sun disc centred in it. Two snapped pale_fluted_column stubs in front. The 3-wide passage is CHOKED WITH SAND up to groundY+5, leaving exactly two blocks of headroom.

ruinLevels.2: the lintel drops into a rubble + brick heap and you climb OVER instead of under — a different and better feeling

lootCache.note: in the lintel void — reachable only by climbing a hash-crumbled tower corner

prefabs.why: noClear (verified: PrefabDef.noClear exists and skips the pre-build clear) is the whole prefab: it must EMERGE from the dune, not stand on a scraped pad. A monumental door to nothing, and a perfect gold sun disc eight blocks above a doorway you have to crawl through on your belly.

prefabs.who: The diggers, conscripted when the spring failed.
prefabs.whatHappened: Sekhat ordered them to dig until they found water. They found the wrong thing — look at the corner of the bottom chamber. This is the Throat in miniature: the shaft that ALMOST opened.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The last basket, still full, still hooked.
prefabs.build: Broken spoil-heap ring of sand/rubble. A 3x3 shaft to a 5x5 chamber at digFloorY(groundY, 7), walls stone with dungeon_masonry ribs. planks treads on log posts spiral the shaft wall, each +1 from the last (the ruined_watchtower step math, already BFS-tested in prefabs.test.ts); the shaft's south column stays open the whole way — that hole IS the jump-arc headroom. A log A-frame windlass over the mouth with a planks bucket hanging one below the crossbeam. THREE ember_crystal set into the stone in the SE corner of the bottom chamber, and the digger's pick leaning on that wall. A bone_block digger under a rubble fall opposite.

ruinLevels.2: the shaft has caved: fill it with rubble from halfway down, seal the chamber, move the cache up into the surface basket — AND THE EMBER GLOW DOES NOT EXIST, because in that variant nobody ever saw it

prefabs.interrupted: The last basket is full and still on the hook. Somebody was coming back up for it.
prefabs.why: NO SPAWN REGION — the shaft is silent, which is the point. They were two swings from the fire that killed the world. The ember glow leaking up the shaft at night is the whole advertisement.

prefabs.who: Nine wards, set in a ring, holding something in the ground.
prefabs.whatHappened: Eight of them still burn.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Nothing. NO LOOT CACHE, DELIBERATELY — cold rune light means danger, per the light table. The payload is the fight.
prefabs.build: A radius-5 ring of 24 rune_plate laid flush at groundY. At the 8 compass points, rune_plate_lit instead, each with a 3-tall pale_ruin_stone menhir standing on it and a chain hanging from its top toward the centre. Interior floored crypt_slate, with a 3x3 of rune_plate_lit at dead centre and a single obsidian block standing on it. THE BREAK: one hash-picked menhir is a rubble stump, its rune_plate_lit swapped for a rune_plate that has itself been split into rubble. Crater is room-flavoured: cinderrift = ash + one lava; crypt_depths = web + two skull_pile; gloomfen = mud + roots. NO torch, NO lantern, NO brazier — the only light is the eight surviving rune_plate_lit (7 each) and it is cold and blue.
prefabs.interrupted: From the broken point, a four-cell trail of rubble and one skull_pile leads OUT of the ring, across the flattened floor and off the footprint. Every other prefab in this batch is a container that failed. This is the one where you can see which direction the contents went.

spawnRegion.note: bind the room's nastiest table here at maxAlive + 1

prefabs.why: The only prefab whose payload is the FIGHT and not a bag, and it justifies that on the light-is-language rule.

prefabs.placement: >=60 blocks off the causeway — finding one is a reward for leaving the road
prefabs.who: A fen-fisher. This decade, not this century.
prefabs.whatHappened: Nothing yet.
prefabs.whatsLeft: His supper, his tackle, and his lantern, which is lit.
prefabs.build: Nine pale_log stilts to deckY. A 5x5 planks platform. A thatch lean-to with a rotting_planks back wall. A drying rack of two log posts and a beam with reeds hung under it (reeds is a non-solid cross — hanging it in air off a beam is legal and reads exactly right). Three plank treads stepping up out of the water from a mud shelf, each +1. A lantern on the ridge. A hay bedroll, a bookshelf (his one book), a cobblestone cooking ring with a torch in it.
prefabs.interrupted: The net is still in the water. Four or five `web` blocks laid FLAT on the murk surface at deckY, spreading away from the platform — a cast net that has not been hauled in. An existing block, one wrong context, and now you hesitate before you swim through it, AND THERE IS NO SPIDER.
prefabs.why: THE CONTROL EXPERIMENT. The only warm prefab in the Gloomfen, existing so that the other eight are cold by comparison. Light is a currency and you can inflate it. It also answers 'why does anyone come here' with the least heroic and most convincing answer available: there are still fish, and someone has to eat. NO SPAWN REGION — this camp is safe, and it is the only safe thing out here.

prefabs.placement: 2 FIXED anchors astride the causeway: z=252 (ruinLevel 0, lantern under the arch) and z=132 (ruinLevel 2, dark)
prefabs.who: The crown's revenue service. Ysmere taxed the temple road.
prefabs.whatHappened: The toll-keepers were the last people on the causeway with a reason to stay, and the water took the ground floor before it took them.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The strongbox they could not carry, and the roof — the only place in the southern fen you can stand and see the Drownbell and the temple in one frame.
prefabs.build: Ground floor: pale_ruin_stone walls, a 3-wide barrel arch straight through along local z — THE ROAD PASSES THROUGH IT. crypt_slate floor. The gate: iron_bars filling the arch EXCEPT a 1-wide gap at the side, rusted half-open (the sundered_city portcullis idiom). Upper floor at FL+4, reached by 4 plank-on-log treads climbing the interior wall, each +1 (watchtower stair math, 2 blocks of headroom over every tread). Roof at FL+8 with alternating merlons and a 1-wide open well over the stair. moss_carpet climbing the first two courses of the seaward face ONLY (the marsh comes from one side).
prefabs.detail: A bookshelf lies TOPPLED outside the arch on the road, in the mud, three blocks from the door, with rubble scattered from it — someone tried to carry the records out and gave up ten paces from the building.

lootCache.note: upper floor, behind the counter — reaching it requires the interior stair, and the climb is what puts you on the roof for the vista

prefabs.why: Because the road runs THROUGH the arch, you cannot cross the fen without walking through a building. That is what makes the causeway read as a road and not a footpath.

scatter.note: CLUSTERED, not spread — a village drowns as a village

prefabs.who: A fen farmer, generations before the flood.
prefabs.whatHappened: The water came up in a single night and it never went back down. The house is still standing, fully furnished, six feet under.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Everything they owned, because they never came back for it.
prefabs.build: Walls rotting_planks from groundY+1 to deckY+2, log corner posts, sitting in 2-3 blocks of murk. Interior floor at groundY (underwater): hay bed, planks table, bookshelf, the cache. Roof: thatch gable to a ridge at deckY+5 with a 2x2 patch of thatch MISSING on the leeward slope — that is your way in, you drop through the roof into the flooded room. Chimney: cobblestone, 3 blocks above the ridge, the tallest thing for 40 metres, capped with moss_carpet. vines off the eaves; reeds crowding the walls at the waterline. An UNLIT lantern lies on the flooded floor (the room's one unexplained light lives at causeway z=96 — see S3 — and only there).
prefabs.interrupted: The door is barred FROM THE INSIDE — iron_bars across the doorway with planks nailed over them, underwater. They did not drown because they could not get out. They drowned because they were keeping something out.
prefabs.why: From the causeway you see a thatch ridge and a chimney standing out of open water with nothing under them, which is a silhouette that asks a question. NO SPAWN REGION — the horror is that this place is empty.

prefabs.who: The temple's lay brothers — the orchard fed the pilgrims.
prefabs.whatHappened: The marsh salted the ground, the trees died standing, and the weavers moved in.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The harvest basket, still half full.
prefabs.build: 16 pale_log trunks on a STRICT 4-block pitch (local 0,4,8,12 x 0,4,8,12), heights 4..6 by ctx.rand — BUT THE PITCH NEVER VARIES. Sparse dead_leaves crowns (2-3 blocks each, not full canopies — they are dead). vines hanging from crowns. web strung between adjacent trunks at crown height (a lattice: one web block on the midpoint of each trunk pair). glow_shroom clustered at the base of every trunk — the orchard fruits now, and what it fruits is light. bone_block scraps at 3 hashed spots. moss_carpet on the north face of each trunk's ground block. A 4-plank ladder against one trunk, and beneath it a basket: a 2x2 ring of planks with hay inside and three glow_shroom growing out of it.

lootCache.note: up the ladder, in the crotch of the tallest trunk. The ladder is +1 treads and works with the engine's 1-block jump.

spawnRegion.note: when placed via `near` for weaver-hollow-w/e, use bindSpawnTable instead so the existing tables re-centre on it

prefabs.why: Nothing in a swamp grows in a 4x4 grid at a fixed pitch. ROWS ARE THE FINGERPRINT OF A HUMAN HAND, AND THEY SURVIVE THE DEATH OF EVERY SINGLE TREE. The cheapest way to say 'people farmed here' in a voxel world, and it costs 16 trunks. He was up the ladder picking when it happened, and the basket is still where he set it down.

prefabs.who: There were nine Vessel Kings and nine colossi.
prefabs.whatHappened: Eight fell. The ninth is the setpiece in the middle of the room; every one of these is a piece of one of Sekhat's brothers, scattered across 480 blocks — so the player assembles the story BY WALKING.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Depends which piece you found. hash2 picks HEAD / HAND / FOOT. Every variant gets a hieroglyph_wall name-tablet bearing a different king.

variants.HEAD: A crowned head lying on its cheek, 7x7x6 sandstone_tomb_brick; nemes lappets are two 2x5 slabs of hieroglyph_wall sweeping back from the temples; crown band with a gold_block. ONE eye socket is a 2x2 recess left empty; the other holds a recessed lantern — half-buried, ONE EYE STILL LIT, STARING SIDEWAYS AT THE HORIZON. The mouth is an open 2x2 hollow you crawl into: web across the lips, the cache inside.
variants.HAND: Fingers curled, palm up, 7 long, 3 tall at the knuckles, rubble at the shattered wrist; a hay bird's nest sits in the palm. DELIBERATELY NO CACHE AND NO SPAWN — finding it teaches you that the desert is not a vending machine.
variants.FOOT: A sandaled foot and a shattered ankle, rubble spill 4 wide. Tucked into the arch of the foot, a tiny shrine: one dungeon_masonry step, one torch (ruinLevel 0 only). Somebody worships here.

prefabs.detail: sand_with_slab apron in a hash-scatter ring around all three.

prefabs.who: The public cistern of Ashkaal — the city's water, free to anyone who could walk down to it.
prefabs.whatHappened: The water fell one terrace a year. Each terrace still carries its tidemark. The last person to walk all the way to the bottom is still down there.
prefabs.whatsLeft: What he was carrying, and what he never came back up for.
prefabs.build: A 9x9 mouth. Six inverted-pyramid terraces: terrace t (0..5) is the ring at inset t+2, walking surface at groundY - t, lined dungeon_masonry. EACH TERRACE'S OUTER COURSE IS ONE BLOCK OF sand_with_slab — that is the tidemark, and it steps down with the terraces so the eye reads a falling water level. Four pale_fluted_column run unbroken from the rim (groundY+2) to the floor: four columns holding up nothing. FLOOR at digFloorY(groundY, 6): cracked_bricks with a 3x3 puddle (mud ring, water 1 deep, rune_plate dead centre UNDER the water). LIGHT: one crystal in a wall niche at the third terrace — the only light, and it is the danger colour, not the safety colour. THE MAN: two bone_block curled at the water's edge with a planks bucket beside him. A log windlass frame at the rim, the rope long gone.
prefabs.detail: He got to the water. THE BUCKET IS FULL.

lootCache.note: in a niche ONE TERRACE ABOVE the floor — you must jump back up a terrace, in the dark, over the slimes

prefabs.who: The Ninth Road Company, the caravan guild that watered the great dig.
prefabs.whatHappened: The aqueduct failed, the road stopped paying, and the last caravan barred the gate FROM THE INSIDE — and then nobody left. Raptors nest in the courtyard now.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The strongroom, still locked, because whatever emptied this place did not want money.
prefabs.build: Perimeter wall of sandstone_bricks 3 tall, one 3-wide gate. Gate posts: pale_fluted_column x4 tall, a banner on each cap, a lantern between them at ruinLevel 0 only. THE BAR: a horizontal log run at the gate, two above the floor — visibly barring it from inside; at ruinLevel 2 one log is broken out (something came through). Courtyard well: 2x2 shaft down 4, lined dungeon_masonry, water at the bottom at ruin 0, dry with a planks bucket on the rim at ruin 1-2. Lean-to stalls of log posts + thatch roofs against the E and W walls. THE CARAVAN: three carts in a line — planks bodies at FL+1, log axle blocks beneath, hay cargo on top. The middle cart is TIPPED, its planks flat on the sand and its hay spilled in a 3-block fan. Cook fire ring of cobblestone with cold ash in the centre, NEVER a torch. STRONGROOM in the NE corner, roofed planks, door is a single iron_bars.
prefabs.detail: The gate is barred from the inside and the courtyard is empty. One cart is half-unloaded — three hay bales on the ground beside it, arranged in the neat row of a man who expected to finish.

prefabs.who: The people who were here before the kingdom.
prefabs.whatHappened: They stacked their dead in courses, faced the mound with pale brick, and sealed it with iron. Something inside pushed the bars OUT.
prefabs.whatsLeft: A cache under a hanging chain.
prefabs.build: A radius-4 dirt disc raised to groundY + max(0, 3 - floor(dist)), capped with moss_carpet. A 3-wide, 3-tall entry passage on local -z, faced with pale_ruin_stone jambs and a single lintel course. Inner chamber (2..6 x 2..6) with its floor ONE STEP DOWN, floored crypt_slate, walls and ceiling pale_temple_brick. Two shelf courses of bone_block along the east and west inner walls with skull_pile scattered on the lower. Two hanging_moss from the lintel and the chamber ceiling. One chain from the ceiling centre, over the cache. LIGHT: a brazier burns at ruinLevel 0; at ruinLevel >=1 THE BRAZIER IS GONE AND A glow_shroom GROWS WHERE IT STOOD. THE GATE: iron_bars fill the passage mouth EXCEPT the two centre cells, which are AIR, and one rubble block sits OUTSIDE.
prefabs.detail: The bars are bent OUT and the rubble lies OUTSIDE the gate. Everything else in the barrow is undisturbed — the shelves are still stacked. NOBODY BROKE IN.
prefabs.demotedWhy: DEVICE DUPLICATES drowned_house (intent inverted: barred from the inside / bars bent out). Kept as plain prose, not a labelled beat.

prefabs.who: Not a tomb. A processing floor.
prefabs.whatHappened: Bodies came in on the boards and went down the pit.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Whatever fell in with them. AND THE PIT IS NOT FULL, which is the part worth thinking about.
prefabs.build: A 5x5 pit dug 4 down (digFloorY), lined sewer_brick, floored bone_block, its bottom two courses alternating bone_block and air (hash-picked ~40% air) so it reads as a heap of loose bone, with 5 skull_pile on top. Over the pit: four pale_log posts and a temple_boards deck at FL+5 with a 1x1 HOLE directly over the pit centre. A temple_boards ramp climbs from ground to the deck (5 treads, each +1). Walls: a 9x9 ring of sewer_brick with hanging_moss on every second lintel and roots reaching down into the pit from the deck's underside.

ruinLevels.0: a brazier burns on the deck corner
ruinLevels.2: the post beneath it has rotted, that corner of the deck is air, and the brazier lies at the pit's edge among the bones, STILL LIT — glow at the bottom of a hole is the best light this engine makes

lootCache.note: IN the pit, on the bones, under the hole in the deck. Verified: stampPrefab computes groundY + local[1], so negative offsets are legal. This session's 3D pickup range means you CANNOT loot it from the deck.

prefabs.detail: TWO CHAINS, THREE BLOCKS LONG, HANGING FROM THE DECK'S UNDERSIDE AND ENDING IN AIR OVER THE HEAP. Whatever was hanging on them is not on them.

prefabs.rewriteRequired: THE PROPOSAL'S BUILD SPEC ARGUES WITH ITSELF MID-PARAGRAPH ('GUARDROOM at lx 1..3? No -') and its guardroom overruns the 13-wide footprint. Rewrite the geometry before coding; the fiction is sound.
prefabs.who: Six cells and a guardroom, dug into the fen by people who thought the fen would stay out.
prefabs.whatHappened: The water came up through the floor. The cells were locked from the corridor side.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The keys, and whatever the last guard was paid with.
prefabs.build: dungeon_masonry shell; the whole interior floor sits one below groundY, flooded one block deep (murk_water in gloomfen, sewer_sludge elsewhere) so PLAYERS WADE IT and mobs wade it purposefully. A central corridor with a temple_boards catwalk down its centre line at FL, so players walk dry-shod above the flood while mobs wade beside them. Six 3x3 cells, three per side, separated by piers; each cell's corridor face is iron_bars with a 1-wide door cell. Cells 1,2,4,5,6 locked. Ceiling dungeon_masonry with 4 hash-picked collapse holes letting light and roots in. Inside each cell: hay in a corner, a chain from the ceiling centre, a skull_pile at the chain's foot. Cell 6's back wall has a 1x2 rubble breach with roots pouring through. Guardroom at the corridor's east head, raised one step (dry): a temple_boards table, a brazier (lit only at ruinLevel 0), a bookshelf.
prefabs.interrupted: Cell 3's door is open, its bar lies in the corridor, and its chain is one block short and ends in NOTHING. Every other cell still holds its prisoner. The guardroom brazier is on the wrong side of a locked door and there is no body in the guardroom.

prefabs.rewriteRequired: The proposal's build text visibly loses track of its own coordinates mid-paragraph ('lz -1.. i.e. off-footprint; instead...'). The geometry needs a rewrite, not a redesign.
prefabs.who: A city drain, and no city.
prefabs.whatHappened: Whatever Valdrenn's engineers built this to carry, they built it to carry a long way. It still runs.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The sump, at the deepest point of the channel.
prefabs.build: A 7-wide, 5-tall sewer_brick bank face, sunk 1 into the bank. A 3-wide, 3-tall barrel-vault mouth, arched with a flat-ish voxel arch (the grid does not do curves and pretending otherwise looks worse). GRATE: iron_bars fill the mouth EXCEPT one cell where three bars have been prised out. A sewer_sludge channel runs inward, sunk one below FL, walls and floor sewer_brick, so the sludge visibly pours toward the mouth. CATWALK: temple_boards along both channel edges with 3 hash-picked planks missing over the sludge. Two chain from the vault ceiling. moss_carpet caps the bank on both flanks; roots dangle from the vault lip; hanging_moss beside them.

ruinLevels.2: collapse the arch's centre course into rubble and let a shaft of skylight in

spawnRegion.note: bind the gloomfen bandit table here — this outfall is how they get in

prefabs.why: Also the prefab that motivates sewer_sludge as a directional map cue: everything downstream is sludge, everything upstream is fen. That single colour change tells the player where to go and who lives there.
prefabs.detail: The grate was cut FROM THE INSIDE, and a lantern is lit on the wrong side of it, past the grate, INSIDE. Somebody is living up that pipe, and they are not coming out through the front door.

prefabs.who: The old kingdom hanged bandits at the crossroads and left them up as advice.
prefabs.whatHappened: The bandits took the road anyway.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Nothing. NO LOOT — a gibbet is a warning, not a prize (the wayshrine rule).
prefabs.build: Two pale_log posts and a crossbeam. From the beam's centre, ONE chain. Below it, an iron_bars cage with a skull_pile inside. A 3x3 of path with one moss_carpet corner. THE SECOND GIBBET: its posts exist but the beam is snapped, one post is short, and its chain lies coiled as three chain blocks FLAT ON THE GROUND — a cage that was TAKEN DOWN, not one that fell. At ruinLevel 2 the standing cage's iron_bars are air and only the chain remains: SOMEBODY GOT OUT.
prefabs.detail: The severed chain. Rust would have left a frayed, tapering end; a clean two-block gap in a chain run says a blade, from below, at night.

prefabs.placement: always in open murk, never against a bank
prefabs.who: The fen people did not bury their dead in the ground, because in a marsh the ground gives them back.
