prefabs.whatHappened: They laid them on rafts with their grave-goods and a candle and pushed them out into the open water. The rafts never sank, because that is not how rafts work, and the candles never went out, because that is not how the Gloomfen works.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The grave goods. Taking them is the single most morally uncomfortable loot in the game, and that is why the table should be trophy-heavy: you are robbing a funeral, and what you get is a keepsake you can only sell.
prefabs.build: Three rafts, each a 3x2 rotting_planks slab floating at deckY: two clustered, one drifted. Each carries a bone_block, a 2-tall pale_log mast with a banner, and a lit bog_candle at the foot. A ring of reeds around the whole footprint — they were pushed out through the reeds and the reeds closed behind them. moss_carpet on the raft edges.
prefabs.detail: The third raft has drifted into the reeds and TIPPED. Half its planks are at deckY; the other half are at groundY under the murk; and the bone_block that was on it is on the bottom of the fen, three blocks away, OUTSIDE its raft. Nothing in the Gloomfen stays where it was put.

prefabs.placement: one FIXED anchor beached diagonally ACROSS the Drowned West Road spur (see S3)
prefabs.who: The river guild. Ysmere shipped stone and grain down the fen channels on flat-bottomed barges.
prefabs.whatHappened: It was under way when the water rose. A marsh has no channel — it grounded on a hummock and broke its back.
prefabs.whatsLeft: The cargo, still lashed down.
prefabs.build: pale_log hull ribs along both gunwales with 3 SNAPPED amidships. rotting_planks deck with lz 7..9 missing entirely — you see straight down into the flooded hold. Hold floor at groundY, underwater: hay bales and planks crates, one crate burst open with its contents on the mud. The mast has FALLEN: lay a pale_log horizontally from the deck out over the water, with a banner tangled at its end, half in the murk. Stern tiller: an iron_bars post.
prefabs.detail: A bone_block on the deck at the tiller, AND ONLY THERE. The steersman never let go of it. Everything else on this boat went into the water; he is still steering.

lootCache.note: in the intact stern hold, underwater — you have to go under the deck

prefabs.why: The long low horizontal silhouette is a deliberate counterweight to the Drownbell's vertical one. A room needs both.

prefabs.who: Nothing built this. Something enormous died here and the pride moved into its bones.
prefabs.whatHappened: A caravan camped in the only shade for a mile.
prefabs.whatsLeft: Their strongbox, chewed open at one corner.
prefabs.build: Two arcs of five bone_block ribs curving inward to meet 5 above; a bone_block spine along the ridge. A hollow 3x3x3 bone_block skull with a 1x2 eye void and web across it. Inside the cage: a nest of hay with dead_leaves and rubble mixed in. Outside: a smashed cart — scattered planks, two log wheels lying flat — and the strongbox half under the ribs. sand_with_slab apron. NO LIGHT.
prefabs.detail: The cart's wheels are 6 blocks apart. IT WAS DRAGGED.

lootCache.note: you loot it INSIDE the nest. The cache position IS the price.

prefabs.why: The only prefab in the desert made by no one, and the one that will actually kill you.

prefabsCut.reason: duplicates setpiece S4's tomb (descending stair, hieroglyph corridor, robbed antechamber, sealed niche, gold sarcophagus, high-tier cache) in the same 480sq room, AND digs to -6 with no bedrock clamp in a room whose groundY runs 7..19 over bedrock at y1. Salvaged into S4: the thin-wall tell, and the lit brazier in the FALSE chambers ('the robbers lit those').

shrine #3 in a document that already has wayshrine (shipped), warding_ring and the Temple. Its one good idea — the ruinLevel light swap lantern -> crystal, the shrine wearing its own warning label — goes to tidewarden_ward.
a miniature of setpiece S2 (roofless, apse, altar, ward plates), and the THIRD unexplained burning light in one room. One unexplained light per room; the Gloomfen's is the lamp at causeway z=96.

setpieces.name: The Drownbell, the Leaning Campanile of Ysmere
setpieces.note: 18 blocks EAST of the causeway so it frames the road rather than blocking it, standing in open murk in the serpent shallows
setpieces.problemItSolves: gloomfen is base 12 / amplitude 2.5 — a flat grey plate with no skyline, which is precisely why the owner says it feels empty. WORLD_HEIGHT is 48 and the terrain uses 15 of it. We are wasting thirty blocks of sky.
setpieces.build: 9x9 pale_ruin_stone plinth y8->y12 (one block proud of the water, a dry landing you can swim to). A 7x7 hollow shaft, walls 1 thick, pale_temple_brick with hash-driven pale_ruin_stone decay bites, y13->y33. THE LEAN: shift the whole shaft +1 in x at y18, y24 and y30 — three offsets over twenty courses, a stepped batter, exactly how a Minecraft builder fakes a lean; it reads perfectly at distance. On the overhanging face, a 1-wide 6-tall air column at y20..y25 knifes dusk light into the stairwell. Interior: 20 planks treads spiralling the wall, one per course, pale_log landing beam every 4th (watchtower stair math, BFS-tested). Window slits every 5 courses. BELFRY y34..y38: four pale_fluted_column corners, open on all four sides, A FLOOR OF iron_bars so you stand on a grate and look straight down 22 blocks of stairwell you just climbed. One bog_candle burning in it.
setpieces.theBell: THE BELL IS NOT IN THE BELFRY. An iron_bars chain hangs three blocks from the roof underside and ends in air. The bell is eight blocks northeast, at (186,158), IN THE WATER: a 7x7x4 dome of gold_block, half-buried in the mud, its top two courses breaking the surface — the biggest, brightest, most valuable-looking object in the entire room, and it is A GRAVE MARKER. Under its lip, a 1-block crawl gap holds three bone_block and a small cache. The last thing anyone in Ysmere heard was this bell, and then it fell on them.
setpieces.hooks: cache_gloomfen in the belfry (top of the climb = top of the reward curve) + the small cache under the bell. A marsh_wisp anchor r10 on the belfry — the lamplighters climbed up here when the water came, and they are still up here.
setpieces.theShot: 27 blocks above the waterline, leaning, with a green light in its head, seen from the Fen Gate across 150m of flat water at fixedTime 0.86.

setpieces.name: The Temple of the Tidewardens
setpieces.replaces: the prefab-stamped 20x16 sunken_temple at (160,48). The prefab stays in the catalog for reuse elsewhere; the Gloomfen's own temple graduates, the way the Sundered City's keep did.
steps: THE PROCESSIONAL GOES UNDERWATER. The causeway ends at z=64. Four crypt_slate steps go DOWN from y12 to y9; the road runs submerged two blocks under the murk for z 60..54; four steps climb back out. You wade, in the dark, for twelve metres, to reach the front door of a church. There is no drowning damage in this engine, so it is pure atmosphere at zero risk. Two toppled pale_fluted_column lie across the flooded channel to clamber over.

THE PLATE. Flatten x144..176 / z34..58 to y11; floor crypt_slate with ~12% of columns broken open to murk_water — ankle-deep marsh INSIDE the sanctuary. The cold blue-grey tile is what makes the water read as a WOUND rather than as terrain. moss_carpet on the dry margins.

THE COLONNADE. pale_fluted_column at 4-block pitch, 6 tall, tops joined by a pale_ruin_stone architrave along the long axis. A THIRD OF THEM TOPPLED — a fallen column is a 6-long horizontal run lying on the floor with its capital two blocks into the water, and you walk along it like a log bridge.
WALLS. pale_temple_brick to y19, hash bites to cracked pale_ruin_stone, moss_carpet on the first two courses, vines every ~5.

THE ROSE WALL, north end above the altar: a 7x5 panel of stained_glass (light 9) in a pale_ruin_stone frame, with THREE rune_plate set into the wall beneath it as a frieze. It is the only warm light for forty metres and it is the last thing Ysmere built.

THE ALTAR AND THE BREACH. A 3-step marble dais at x156..163 / z36..40. On it, a ring of 8 rune_plate — THE SAME RING THE PLAYER HAS NOW SEEN SIX TIMES OUT IN THE FEN — cracked open, with a 2x2 shaft of air punched straight through the middle of it.

THE UNDER-VAULT. Below the breach, a 9x9x5 chamber (y5..y10), pale_temple_brick walls (STILL CLEAN — this room never flooded until the seal broke), crypt_slate floor, filled to y11 with murk_water so the breach is a well. YOU DIVE. At the bottom: a ring of blue_crystal (light 11) whose glow rises up through the flooded shaft and pools on the nave floor, so you SEE the vault before you understand it — the light is the invitation and the warning, in the same block. On a pale_ruin_stone plinth at the centre: cache_gloomfen, the biggest cache in the room. Around the walls, six iron_bars alcoves, each with a bone_block. The Tidewardens went down here and sealed themselves in with whatever they were guarding, and one of them pried a ward plate loose to do it.

THE NORTH DOOR IS THE PORTAL. gloomfen-city-north sits at (160,30), thirty blocks past the altar. The temple's north wall has a collapsed section beside the Rose Wall, and that breach is the road to Valdrenn. You cross the fen, you take the temple, and you leave Ysmere through the hole the marsh made.
setpieces.hooks: recentre the shipped `temple-guard` table on the nave (the lizardmen are not invaders — they are the fen's river-folk and this is their church). Reserve an unbound spawnRegion anchor at (160,38) r4 on the dais for a future named lizardman.

setpieces.name: The Lamplighters' Road
setpieces.file: voxelstructures.ts (rework of buildGloomfen's GLOOMFEN_CAUSEWAY, x=160, z 58..304)
setpieces.notNewGeometry: Today the causeway is `planks` and `path` with a hash-driven missing-plank gradient — a mechanic pretending to be a story.

stretches.name: the Planking
stretches.material: planks on pale_log posts, pale_ruin_stone kerbstones every 8

stretches.name: the Rot
stretches.note: gaps <=2 (jumpable); place a solid block at y=groundY under every gap so a miss is a wade, never a death

lamps.lightRule: A HARD FUNCTION OF Z, NOT OF HASH: lantern (warm, 13) for z >= 240; bog_candle (green, 8) for 240 > z >= 144; DARK for z < 144. Walk north and the road tells you, in light, exactly how far you are from anything that loves you.
lamps.theException: THE LAMP AT z=96 IS LIT. Not a corpse-candle. A `lantern`. Warm, tended, recently. Ninety-six blocks deep into a dead marsh, past the rot, past the last green flame, somebody has been out here with oil. Nothing in the room explains it, no NPC mentions it, no quest turns on it. IT IS JUST LIT. This is the room's one unexplained light. Do not add a second.

setpieces.tollhouses: astride the road at z=252 (ruinLevel 0, lantern under the arch) and z=132 (ruinLevel 2, dark). You walk through both, and the second one is the moment the fen stops being scenery.
setpieces.drownedWestRoad: The second city portal at (52,92) currently has NO ROAD AT ALL, which is why that whole quadrant reads as empty. Spur west from a junction at (160,110) to (56,92): rotting_planks, missChance 0.35 (it is nearly gone), two DARK lamplighter_posts, a beached barge_wreck lying diagonally ACROSS the spur so you climb over its broken back to continue, and a tidewarden_ward at ruinLevel 2 where the spur crosses the deepest water.
setpieces.engineCost: ZERO new systems. The lamps and tollhouses are fixed-anchor stampPrefab calls (the pattern buildGloomfen already uses for sunken_temple); the material stretches are three branches in the existing per-z loop; the spur is a second copy of that loop on a different axis. Update authoredExclusions().

setpieces.name: The Colossus of Sekhat the Ninth, and the Tomb Beneath
setpieces.form: A SEATED colossus, buried to the chest, FACING SOUTH — staring at you the entire walk up from the hub gate. Shoulders 24 blocks across, head top at groundY+28 (ground ~13-16 here, so ~41 of 48 — legal, with clearance). Skin sandstone_tomb_brick; nemes headdress lappets are broad slabs of hieroglyph_wall falling to the shoulders; crown band with a gold_block sun disc at the brow. Face cut with E8's ASCII plate helper.
setpieces.theEyes: THE EYES ARE THE LANDMARK. Both sockets are 3x2 recesses one block deep with `lantern` (light 13) set at the back. At night, from anywhere in the southern half of a 480sq room, THE SUNSCOUR HAS EXACTLY TWO LIGHTS IN IT AND THEY ARE LOOKING AT YOU. Nothing in this document earns its block cost like these two lanterns. (This is the desert's one unexplained light.)
tomb: 1. THE PROCESSIONAL — 3 wide, 24 long, hieroglyph_wall both sides, a lantern every 6 blocks. ONE LANTERN IS OUT, and the dark it leaves is where the corridor turns.
2. THE HALL OF DIGGERS — 13x11, bone_block shelving in three rows: he was buried with his workmen. web in every corner. spawnRegion: skeleton + restless_bones, maxAlive 8.

3. THE CISTERN BREACH — the tomb was cut straight through an older cistern and the builders just walled around it. A 9x9 chamber at bedrock+1, a real 5x5 water pool one deep with mud at the edge. THE LAST WATER IN THE SUNSCOUR, SEALED UNDER A STATUE. Slimes. One crystal over the pool.

4. THE VESSEL CHAMBER — hieroglyph_wall walls, four pale_fluted_column floor to ceiling, a rune_plate inlaid dead centre, and on a 3-tier dais a gold_block sarcophagus flanked by four canopic pylons. cache_desert at rare+ minRarity. SEKHAT SPAWNS HERE. Salvaged from the cut tomb prefab: the REAL chamber sits behind a hieroglyph_wall course ONE BLOCK THINNER than its neighbours, and the three false chambers off the Hall are all robbed (lids off, gold_block missing, one skeleton each) WITH A LIT BRAZIER IN EACH. The robbers lit those. The real chamber is lit by rune_plate_lit alone, and the sarcophagus lid is ON.

5. THE ROBBERS' SHAFT — a collapsed vertical hole out of the Hall of Diggers, planks treads up a rubble chimney, surfacing ON THE COLOSSUS'S LAP. You come out of the tomb twenty blocks in the air on the knees of a god-king with the whole room laid out beneath you. That is the reward for the descent, and it costs about forty blocks.
setpieces.interrupted: On the INSIDE of the sealed door, a single bone_block skeleton lies with its hands against the stone. Sekhat sealed his diggers in with him. ONE OF THEM CHANGED HIS MIND.

setpieces.name: The Great Aqueduct of Ashkaal, and the Throat it drank from
setpieces.thesis: The answer to 'a 480sq room needs landmarks visible from far away, and a reason to walk toward each one.' The aqueduct IS the reason: a raised road, 8 blocks above the dunes, that physically connects all three landmarks IN THE ORDER THE STORY HAPPENED. Follow it and you cannot get lost. Leave it and you are in the desert.

route.note: broken terminus over the OASIS — the spring's surface mouth. The last 20 blocks of channel are SNAPPED OFF and lie in pieces on the sand below, so the aqueduct visibly no longer reaches the water.

arrives at the Colossus's south porch. The final 20 blocks widen into a 7-wide ground-level causeway of sandstone_bricks flanked by buried_pylon-scale gate stubs. THIS IS THE PROCESSIONAL. You walk it whether you meant to or not.
it simply ENDS — the last twenty blocks fell into the Throat when the ground gave way. The snapped channel hangs over the pit.

setpieces.routeNote: Four STRAIGHT legs. No diagonals — a diagonal masonry run staircases and reads as a mistake.
setpieces.construction: sandstone_bricks pier pairs every 5th block up to deckY = groundY+8; a 3-wide sandstone_tomb_brick channel deck; side rails taking hash ruin-bites. THREE AUTHORED BREAKS at (280,350), (246,300), (246,170) — each a 5-7 block gap with a rubble field beneath. THE BREAKS ARE THE GAMEPLAY: the deck is the only fast, mob-free travel line across 480 blocks, and you pay for it in jumps. Put a cache on the far side of each break. Author five deathwatch_post prefabs at pier bases along the run: the road WAS patrolled, and technically still is.

theThroat.detail: This is where Sekhat's diggers broke through. The walls are exposed strata and they are the geology lesson: sand at the rim, sandstone, stone, dark_stone, obsidian at the bottom. A 3-wide spiral ledge descends counterclockwise — a real, walkable path, hash-decayed so two sections have collapsed and must be jumped. On the ledge: the diggers' log/planks scaffolds, hay baskets, a windlass frame. At the floor, an obsidian-lipped fissure with a lava pool and ember_crystal (light 12) studding the rim. A rune_plate is set into the Throat's north lip — the diggers sealed it, afterwards, and it did not help.
theThroat.boneRoad: From the north rim, a bone road of bone_block and ash runs to the existing Cinderrift portal at (144,32), deliberately echoing the bone road that already exists INSIDE the Cinderrift. The two rooms are the same wound seen from either side.
theThroat.loot: high-tier cache at the fissure lip, no owner-lock: the most dangerous three blocks in the room.

setpieces.theShot: AT NIGHT THE ROOM HAS EXACTLY TWO LIGHTS: the Colossus's lantern eyes in the south, and a forty-block amber glow rising out of the Throat in the north. You can stand on the aqueduct deck and see both. That is the whole desert, told in two glows and a raised road.

setpiecesDeferred.name: The Ossuary of Nine Hundred
setpiecesDeferred.reason: Scored well (bone walls stacked in courses BECAUSE the builders ran out of stone). Deferred purely for scope — the Gloomfen already gets three setpieces this pass. The natural home for the crypt's re-sprited faction if the fen ever wants undead.

orderOfOperations: E0 FIRST, before any screenshot of anything. Eight shipped bandits are standing on black coins in the owner's client right now, and six of this roster's sprites would join them.
E3 before any prefab cache. Every desert and dungeon cache pays forest loot today.

E1 before Batch 3. Four agents converged on it independently; it is thirty lines; and without it 'the same mob is genuinely different deep' means 'the same mob has more hp' — the failure this whole exercise was convened to avoid.
E2 before Batch 6. Do not ship a splitter until minions grant nothing.
The nightmare_run_1 loader check before Batch 7. Ten minutes now, or a horse in halves later.

cuts.reason: L11 rank adds greater_frost (manaCost 14), L15 adds greater_heal (28). combat.ts:48 refuses a mana ability on a mob AND returns before setting a cooldown, so chooseAttack re-picks it every tick: a permanent whiff loop, not a fight. Also healer #6, and a six-tailed kitsune saint in a biome whose spine is soldiers who were never told to disband.

Four of the desert's eight mobs are drawn HOVERING with a transparent gap above a painted shadow. There is no hover. Alpha-trim drops them to the sand: the skulls roll, the djinn are buried to the waist. Blocked on E9 (a half-day of client work presented as 'zero engine work'). lamp_thrall is the best character beat proposed — bring it back the day E9 lands.

These ARE the shipped `bandit`, `bandit_enforcer` and `hollow_cowl` — same sprite cells, same roles, same rank arcs. Re-authoring a mob at level nine is exactly the failure the ranks system exists to prevent, committed inside a proposal whose headline was the ranks system. Gloomfen ALREADY respawns them via the drowned-company table.

HARD ID COLLISION. `powder_brigand` already exists in shared/mobs.json at L5/hp82 with powder_flask/oil_flask/fuse_line already in abilities.json. This entry redefined the id at L10/hp175 with powder_bomb/powder_keg/firepot_line — three new abilities duplicating three shipped ones. Nothing survived contact with the registry.
Merged into forge_ward (same gargoyle_1 sheet, same statue gimmick). Cinderrift's fiction is better integrated; the desert's kit is better. forge_ward has both.

Four of SIX independently-authored 'harmless thing that runs away', on top of the shipped stolen_goat. One ambient creature per outdoor room. Kept: glimmereye, whose eyes are the exact orange of a torch flame and which therefore LIES to the player.

A giant squirrel throwing acorns in a biome whose spine is a war-broken sapper company honouring a contract nobody is paying. Mechanically charming, tonally a different game. The Greenmarch already ships eight mobs; it is the ONE room the owner says doesn't feel empty.

140-200ms windups at cooldown 0. Their own author wrote 'there is no dodge window; there is only killing it first' — an argument for why the mob shouldn't exist. This engine's entire combat contract is that the telegraphed windup IS the dodge window. Replaced by the shipped spider_bite (320ms, canMoveWhile, dot).

mend_company_greater, greater_bone_mend, wish_of_wholeness, forge_mend_greater, rime_shard_greater, slime_split_greater, greater_gaze, pride_roar_greater, slag_gorge_greater, powder_keg, acorn_barrage, mire_company_greater — twelve abilities whose only diff from their parent was two numbers, because ranks cannot scale a payload. One agent said 'revisit at the fourth _greater.' We were at twelve. This roster ships ZERO. Either take E10 or don't write them.

'A djinn bound in a well, three wishes' explains nothing outside its own room and needs two hovering sprites. Ashkaal — a kingdom that dug for water and broke into the fire underneath — explains the aqueduct, the oasis, the skeletons, the raptors AND the Cinderrift portal. Each fiction was judged on whether it reaches out and makes a neighbouring room mean more.

Two agents wrote incompatible cosmologies for the same room. Ysmere is richer, and vassalage keeps Grelmoss's royal_seal receipt working: Ysmere paid tax to Valdrenn, and the last convoy went into the water.

cuts.id: six of eight new pillars abilities
cuts.reason: firepot_line, withering_gaze, greater_gaze, wish_flame, grave_pillars, ember_trail(desert variant). Four agents independently decided pillars-on-trash was 'the single most valuable thing in this proposal', simultaneously. If every biome's trash marches a line of fire, pillars stops being a signature and becomes wallpaper — and all eight render through the same hardcoded fire4 flipbook, so the crypt would cast the Furnace Golem's magic. Kept: magma_vents and ember_trail, both FIRE, in FIRE rooms, alongside the shipped throne_flames and fuse_line. No E11 needed.

cuts.id: nine of ten new allyHeal abilities
cuts.reason: Five agents each declared their headline mechanic to be 'the first interruptible mob cast in the game'. mend_kin has shipped with interruptible:true since commit 1a417c3. Kept exactly TWO new ones, because they are genuinely different designs: forge_mend (interruptible:FALSE — 'she must not be alive when the heal comes up') and slag_gorge (allyHeal radius 2.5 + includeSelf = a gated SELF-heal, zero engine work).

ward_plate = tomb_seal = rune_plate; tomb_brick = dungeon_masonry (IDENTICAL cell [45,32]); gold_tomb_brick = sandstone_tomb_brick (IDENTICAL cell [41,32]); crypt_slate_tile = crypt_slate; hieroglyph_brick = hieroglyph_wall; plus gold_frieze (a 2-tall wall half that cannot tile alone), sandstone_fluted_column, rippled_sand, urn. Thirty-five proposed names -> twenty-two ids. blocks.json ids are APPEND-ONLY; this mistake would have been permanent.

Eight of eight Gloomfen prefabs and eight of eight crypt prefabs shipped with a labelled 'interrupted action'. A good rule became a tic. SEVEN of twenty-one carry one here: tidewarden_ward, deathwatch_post, digger_shaft, stilt_fisher_camp, warding_ring, drowned_house, sunken_gaol (+ roadside_gibbet, charnel_scaffold, sunscour_caravanserai, sewer_outfall each carry one because it IS the prefab). When every ruin has a severed chain, no severed chain means anything.

cuts.id: three unexplained lights in one room
cuts.reason: The Gloomfen was given lamplighter z=96 (a warm tended lantern past the last green flame), drowned_house's rafter lantern, AND drowned_reliquary's two eternal braziers. Three mysteries dilute to zero. ONE per room: the fen's is z=96, the desert's is the Colossus's lit eye. drowned_reliquary is cut; drowned_house's lantern is now unlit, on the flooded floor.

prefabsInterruptedActionRule: SEVEN of twenty-one carry a labelled `interrupted` action, and each uses a DISTINCT device: tidewarden_ward (an object moved inward = sabotage), deathwatch_post (posture), digger_shaft (an unfinished task, doomed), stilt_fisher_camp (an unfinished task, safe), warding_ring (a trail showing the direction of departure), drowned_house (intent inverted), sunken_gaol (an absence). Every other prefab keeps its observation as plain `detail` prose. Eight of eight Gloomfen prefabs and eight of eight crypt prefabs shipped with one in the proposals; a good rule became a tic. When every ruin has a severed chain, no severed chain means anything.
